question_text: Which statement best describes the arrangement of particles in a solid?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are far apart and move randomly in all directions.

options.label: B
options.text: Particles are tightly packed in a fixed pattern and vibrate in fixed positions.

options.label: C
options.text: Particles are loosely packed and slide past each other.

options.label: D
options.text: Particles are spread out with large gaps between them.

explanation: In solids, particles are arranged in a tight, ordered pattern and can only vibrate around fixed positions. This is why solids have a fixed shape and volume. Options A, C, and D describe gases or liquids instead.
